zh.Newcastle,Northumberland, England: 英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William James Bostock於大約1310年出生在英國爾保斯托時，他的父親Adam de Bostock41歲，母親Joan De Brereton25歲。他於1340年與Alice Maude Moulton在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1372年在他的家鄉享年63歲。

zh-hans.Newcastle,Northumberland, England: 英国英格兰諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William James Bostock于大约1310年出生在英国爾保斯托时，他的父亲Adam de Bostock41岁，母亲Joan De Brereton25岁。他于1340年与Alice Maude Moulton在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1372年在他的家乡享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bostock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Bostock in Cheshire (Botestoch in Domesday Book), so named with an Old English personal name Bōta (see Bott ) + Old English stoc ‘place’.

variants.name: Bostwick
meanings: English (Yorkshire): in northwestern England perhaps a variant of Bostock , and in southeastern England a variant of Bastick (see Bostick 1) with rounding of the vowel.

variants.name: Bott
meanings: English: nickname from Old French bot ‘toad’ (compare Bottrell ). Alternatively, from the Middle English personal name Botte (a survival of Old English Botta). Compare Botting .
South German: occupational name for a messenger, from Middle High German bote ‘messenger, emissary’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William James Bostock was born about 1310, in Bostock, Cheshire, England, his father, Adam de Bostock, was 41 and his mother, Joan De Brereton, was 25. He married Alice Maude Moulton in 1340, in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1372, in his hometown, at the age of 63.

fr.Newcastle,Northumberland, England: Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William James Bostock est né environ 1310, à Bostock, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Adam de Bostock, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Joan De Brereton, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Alice Maude Moulton en 1340, à Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1372, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.Newcastle,Northumberland, England: Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William James Bostock um 1310 in Bostock, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adam de Bostock 41 und seine Mutter Joan De Brereton 25. Er hat 1340 in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom Alice Maude Moulton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1372 im Alter von 63 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

Lui sposato Alice Maude Moulton nel 1340, nel Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1372, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 63 anni.

ko.Newcastle,Northumberland, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니
ko.lifeSummary: Sir William James Bostock(이)가 대략 1310년 영국 체실르 보스톡에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam de Bostock(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan De Brereton(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니에서 Alice Maude Moulton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1372년 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Maude Moulton в 1340 г. в местности нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1372 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 63 лет.
